audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition, with Septuagint Psalm 8 and other citation texture; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for warning, humanity crowned, Jesus made lower than messengers, shared flesh/blood, death defeated, merciful faithful high priest.

verses.restored: Because of this it is necessary for us to pay more abundant attention to what was heard, lest we drift away.
For if the word spoken through messengers became firm, and every transgression and disobedience received just repayment,
how will we escape if we neglect so great salvation/healing, which took beginning of being spoken through the Lord and was confirmed to us by those who heard,
the Divine bearing witness together by signs, wonders, various powers, and distributions of holy Spirit according to his will?
For not to messengers did he arrange the inhabited world to come, concerning which we speak.
But someone testified somewhere, saying: What is human that you remember him, or son of human that you visit him?
You made him a little lower than messengers; with glory and honor you crowned him.
You arranged all things under his feet. For in arranging all things under him, he left nothing unarranged. Yet now we do not yet see all things arranged under him.
But we see Jesus, made a little lower than messengers because of suffering of death, crowned with glory and honor, so that by grace of Divine he might taste death on behalf of everyone.

For it was fitting for him, because of whom are all things and through whom are all things, bringing many children into glory, to complete the pioneer/founder of their salvation/healing through sufferings.
For the one sanctifying and those being sanctified are all from one; because of this he is not ashamed to call them siblings,
saying: I will announce your name to my siblings; in midst of assembly I will hymn you.
And again: I will trust in him. And again: Look, I and the children whom the Divine gave me.

Therefore, since the children have shared in blood and flesh, he himself likewise shared the same things, so that through death he might make inactive the one having power of death, that is, the slanderer/adversary,
and release these, as many as through fear of death were held in slavery through all their life.
For surely he does not take hold of messengers, but he takes hold of seed of Abraham.

Therefore he owed in all things to be made like the siblings, so that he might become merciful and trustworthy high priest in things toward the Divine, to make atoning mercy for the sins/misalignments of the people.
For in what he himself has suffered, being tested, he is able to help those being tested.

translation_notes: Psalm 8 is read christologically but also preserves human vocation: all things not yet seen subject.
Jesus shares blood and flesh; incarnation is solidarity, not escape from embodiment.
High priest language begins as mercy, trustworthiness, and help for tested people.
safety_note: Hebrews 2 uses warning language, but its pastoral center is release from fear of death and merciful help for tested humans.
